expectations.text: Response contains required substrings (dependent attribute, secondary, .proj(, recording_id, BrainRegion, Electrode, IntervalList, Raw, SortGroup, SpikeSortingRecordingSelection, SpikeSortingSelection).
Explains the rule: * refuses when shared attribute is secondary on both sides
expectations.evidence: '* (the natural join) refuses to combine two tables when a shared attribute is secondary on both sides ... If the same name was reached by two different FK paths, DataJoint cannot guarantee the two columns mean the same thing'.

expectations.text: Identifies nwb_file_name as the specific collision and names FK paths
expectations.evidence: 'on SpikeSortingSelection: via -> IntervalList ... on SpikeSortingRecordingSelection: via -> Raw, -> SortGroup, and -> IntervalList'.

expectations.text: Recommends .proj('recording_id') as canonical/primary fix, with two-restriction split as debug-friendly alternative
expectations.evidence: 'There are two correct fixes. They are not equivalent: prefer the first.' Fix 1 is .proj('recording_id'); Fix 2 the split. Explicitly: 'Use it as a debug move, not as the production form'.

expectations.text: Notes error fires at query-build time, not runtime
expectations.evidence: 'It fires the moment you write the * — before any restriction is evaluated, before any rows are fetched. So restricting harder ... cannot help'.

expectations.text: Points to common_mistakes.md § 9 (or equivalent location) for the full rule
expectations.evidence: Baseline cannot literally cite the .md file but the substantive rule is correctly stated; treated as substantive routing per grader philosophy.

expectations.text: Does not misdiagnose as under-specified restriction, data-missing, or merge-table issue
expectations.evidence: Explicitly: 'Do not assume sorting_id is too loose. The error fires before any row is matched'; 'Do not interpret this as no matching rows. That would be an empty fetch, not a DataJointError'.

expectations.text: Does not propose 'use & instead of *' as the fix
expectations.evidence: 'Do not use & instead of *. & is restriction, not join — it doesn't bring in the right side's attributes'.

expectations.text: Does not blame the restriction key
